task_completion.description: Draw a filled green blob character.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A green, blob-like shape is visible in the main drawing area/canvas of OpenToonz.

task_completion.description: Generate a mesh for the blob using the Plastic Tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A triangular mesh overlay is visible on the green blob, indicating the Plastic Tool's mesh generation was applied.

task_completion.description: Build a skeleton structure with at least two bones on the mesh.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least two connected bone lines/nodes (Plastic Tool skeleton) are visible over the mesh in the OpenToonz viewer.

task_completion.description: Animate the skeleton to produce a squash and stretch deformation over 24 frames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Keyframes are visible in the Xsheet/timeline spanning up to frame 24, and the blob appears deformed (squashed or stretched) in the viewer compared to its original shape.

task_completion.description: Save the scene file to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Scene' dialog showing the path '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/slime_rig.tnz', or the OpenToonz window title displaying 'slime_rig.tnz'.

task_completion.description: Render the animation as an MP4 to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An Output Settings dialog showing MP4 format and the path '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/slime_idle.mp4', a rendering progress bar, or a file manager showing the generated 'slime_idle.mp4' file.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to create the animation rather than writing scripts or modifying files via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the OpenToonz interface (drawing tools, Plastic Tool, timeline, render dialogs).

integrity.description: The agent actually drew the blob and created the mesh, rather than just downloading a pre-made file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Intermediate steps showing the drawing process, mesh generation dialog, and manual bone placement in OpenToonz.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually write the .tnz XML file in a text editor to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) open with XML code containing 'MeshLevel' or 'PlasticSkeletonDeformation'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected scene file path is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/slime_rig.tnz'. The expected rendered video path is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/slime_idle.mp4'. The video must be at least 10 KB in size.
